name: io.github.KevinRabun/judges
title: Judges Panel
description: 35 judges that evaluate AI-generated code for security, cost, and quality with built-in AST.

tools.name: evaluate_code
tools.description: Submit code to the full 35-judge tribunal for security, cost, and quality analysis with optional inline configuration.

tools.name: evaluate_code_single_judge
tools.description: Run a single judge against code for targeted domain analysis (e.g. cybersecurity, cost-effectiveness).

tools.name: evaluate_project
tools.description: Multi-file project analysis with cross-file architectural detection (duplication, dependency cycles, inconsistent error handling).

tools.name: evaluate_diff
tools.description: Evaluate only changed lines in a diff — filters findings to affected lines for PR reviews.

tools.name: analyze_dependencies
tools.description: Analyze dependency manifests (package.json, requirements.txt, Cargo.toml, etc.) for supply-chain risks and version pinning issues.

tools.name: get_judges
tools.description: List all available judges with their IDs, names, and domains.

tools.name: evaluate_v2
tools.description: V2 context-aware tribunal evaluation with policy profiles, confidence scoring, and structured context ingestion.

tools.name: evaluate_app_builder_flow
tools.description: 3-step app-builder workflow: tribunal review, plain-language risk translation, and prioritized remediation tasks.

tools.name: evaluate_public_repo_report
tools.description: Clone a public repository URL, run the full judges panel, and generate a consolidated markdown report.

prompts.name: full-tribunal
prompts.description: Convene all 35 judges for a comprehensive LLM-powered deep review.

prompts.name: judge-{id}
prompts.description: Individual judge prompts (35 total) — use a specific judge's expert persona for targeted LLM-powered analysis.
